name: Grip & Release Test
description: 10-second test measuring grip cycle speed, rhythm, and coordination. The participant holds the phone in their palm and repeatedly grips and releases using 3+ fingers.
note: Field names in 'redcap_field' reflect the recommended REDCap data dictionary names. The current field-maps.ts uses shorter shared names (e.g. 'local_uuid', 'device_os') that will conflict when both instruments are imported into REDCap. Update field-maps.ts to use the prefixed names below before connecting to the live REDCap project.

fields.description: Unique identifier for this session. Used for deduplication on re-sync.
UTC timestamp of when the test began.
Actual recorded duration of the test. Should be approximately 10000 ms.
Number of complete grip-and-release cycles recorded during the test.
Grip cycles per second. Primary performance metric. Higher = faster.
Coefficient of variation of inter-grip intervals. Lower = more regular rhythm. Expressed as a percentage (0–100+).
fields.note: Stored as CV not inverted — lower values indicate better regularity. The app displays this inverted (as a regularity score) but the raw CV is what is stored.

fields.description: Which hand the participant used for the test. Selected by participant in setup screen.
Self-reported fatigue level at the time of assessment. Null if not answered.
Whether the participant had taken their medication on the day of assessment. Selected in setup screen. May be null if skipped.
Whether the participant or clinician flagged this session as potentially unreliable on the results screen.
Free-text reason provided when session is flagged. Null if not flagged.

Full array of raw touch events recorded during the session, serialised as JSON. Each event: { t: number (ms from start), x: 0, y: 0, type: 'start'|'end', touch_id: number, rejected: boolean, reject_reason: string|null }. x/y are 0 for grip (position not relevant).
SHA-256 checksum of the raw_data array for integrity verification.
Operating system and version of the device.
Screen (viewport) width at time of assessment.
Screen (viewport) height at time of assessment.
Version of the MAC Spine app that recorded the session.
